title: Webhook Reconciliation Governance
purpose: Define webhook idempotency, ordering, and reconciliation semantics for hosted auth provider synchronization. Webhooks are reconciliation; lazy provisioning is primary.
architectural_constraints: Out-of-order webhooks: delayed retry with exponential backoff
Reconciliation job scans for orphaned auth identities daily
Webhook processing is async (202 response); sync is eventual

deliverables.description: Redis-backed idempotency key store with TTL
Daily scan for orphaned identities + backfill
Documentation: webhook semantics, idempotency, ordering, failure modes

acceptance_criteria: Duplicate webhook (same auth_provider + event_id) ignored within 24h
Out-of-order webhook (update before create) retried with backoff
Reconciliation job detects identity.users rows missing for active auth users
Reconciliation backfill creates missing rows with correct auth_provider
Webhook contract doc covers all event types and failure scenarios

failure_modes.scenario: Redis idempotency store evicts key early

failure_modes.mitigation: Use auth provider API to verify user still exists before backfill
